author: wemeetagain
content: This is a good point. This originally came up bc <@518625292747669516> messaged me that Lighthouse was soon to [disable mplex by default](https://github.com/sigp/lighthouse/pull/9365) in an upcoming release. And so discussion about which transports the protocol mandates is back on the table. I tend to agree with you that keeping a simple and battle-tested fallback is beneficial, but you'd need to convince Lighthouse folks of that. Imo the most important item is just to elevate QUIC in the specs. My PR intentionally states that the fallback SHOULD be there, not MUST. And with all of the p2p discussions, the desire is to use QUIC more directly in the future, so I see heavier reliance on QUIC enshrined now being a good stepping stone in that direction.

author: wemeetagain
content: I think all clients support QUIC now, but we should inventory all clients to ensure this is already the case.

author: tersec
content: Nimbus doesn't quite yet, but will soon. The support needs to be turned on by default
